chapter_title.en: Chapter on What Allah {swt}, the Almighty, Has Made Permissible and Prohibited in Marriage

text: 4445 - وَ رَوَى اِبْنُ أَبِي عُمَيْرٍ عَنْ عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ بْنِ سِنَانٍ عَنْ أَبِي عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ قَالَ: سَأَلْتُهُ عَنِ اَلْمَرْأَةِ تَضَعُ أَ يَحِلُّ أَنْ تَتَزَوَّجَ قَبْلَ أَنْ تَطْهُرَ قَالَ "نَعَمْ وَ لَيْسَ لِزَوْجِهَا أَنْ يَدْخُلَ بِهَا حَتَّى تَطْهُرَ ".

en.bab-ul-qaim-publications:
Hadith.4445 - Ibn Abi Umair narrated from Abdullah ibn Sinan, who narrated from Abu Abdullah (as): I asked him about a woman who gives birth, does it become permissible for her to marry before she purifies (from postnatal bleeding)?
Imam (as) replied: "Yes, it is permissible. However, her husband must not consummate the marriage with her until she purifies."
